researched_category.notes: Open-source CRM platform, #1 on GitHub, designed for technical teams.

one_liner.value: Open-source CRM platform for technical teams to build custom CRM solutions with REST/GraphQL APIs and native AI agent integration
one_liner.notes: Twenty is described as 'the #1 Open Source CRM' that gives technical teams building blocks for custom CRM.

methods.notes: API keys for scripts/internal tools; OAuth 2.0 with authorization code + PKCE for user-facing apps and client credentials for server-to-server. Also supports OAuth for MCP.

access.notes: Pricing page states 'Start your free trial today without credit card.' API is available on both Pro ($9/user/mo) and Organization ($19/user/mo) plans. Self-hosting is free (open-source). No phone/email verification gates observed.

summary.value: Twenty provides well-documented REST and GraphQL APIs with a schema-per-tenant design. Core API (/rest/, /graphql/) for CRUD on records (People, Companies, Opportunities, custom objects). Metadata API (/rest/metadata/, /metadata/) for schema management (objects, fields, relations). Webhooks are supported. Rate limits: 100 req/min (Organization) or 50 req/min (Pro). API keys generated in Settings > API & Webhooks.

agent_interface.notes: Twenty ships a native MCP server built into every Cloud workspace (and self-hosted instance). Official documentation at docs.twenty.com/developers/extend/mcp. Also has twenty-sdk for building custom apps with TypeScript. Several community MCP servers exist on GitHub.

rationale.value: Twenty is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented public REST/GraphQL API, native MCP server, self-serve free trial access (no credit card required), API key and OAuth authentication, and an open-source codebase. There are no material blockers for a developer to create a programmatic agent integration.
rationale.notes: All prerequisites for an agent toolkit are met: public API, self-serve credentials, multiple auth options, and official MCP support.

s1.quote:
Twenty | #1 Open Source CRM

Build your Enterprise CRM at AI Speed

Twenty gives technical teams the building blocks for a custom CRM
s1.title: Twenty homepage

s2.quote: REST and GraphQL APIs generated from your workspace schema. ... Core API — /rest/ and /graphql/ CRUD on records: People, Companies, Opportunities, your custom objects. ... Metadata API — /rest/metadata/ and /metadata/ Schema management: create/modify/delete objects, fields, and relations.
s2.title: APIs - Twenty Documentation

s3.quote: Twenty implements OAuth 2.0 with authorization code + PKCE for user-facing apps and client credentials for server-to-server access. ... Create an API key in Settings → API & Webhooks → + Create key.
s3.title: OAuth - Twenty Documentation

s4.quote: Build apps, use the API, self-host, or contribute to the codebase. Extend Twenty with custom objects, server-side logic, UI components, and AI agents — all as TypeScript packages. REST and GraphQL APIs, webhooks, and OAuth.
s4.title: Developers - Twenty Documentation

s5.quote: Create your first Twenty app in minutes. ... The scaffolder generates a TypeScript project with a starter application-config.ts, a default role, CI/CD workflows, and an integration test.
s5.title: Quick Start - Apps - Twenty Documentation

s6.quote: Twenty exposes an MCP server so that AI assistants — Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, ChatGPT, and others — can read and write your CRM data through natural language. ... MCP is currently in alpha and is only available on some workspaces.
s6.title: MCP Server - Twenty Documentation

s7.quote: Start your free trial today without credit card. ... Pro $9/user/month ... Organization $19/user/month ... REST & GraphQL API: Yes ... Webhooks: Yes ... MCP server: Yes
s7.title: Twenty CRM Pricing

s8.quote: Twenty can be self-hosted on your own infrastructure, giving you full control over your data and deployment.
s8.title: Self-Host - Twenty Documentation

s9.quote: Cloud: https://{your-workspace-url}/mcp (e.g. https://mycompany.twenty.com/mcp) | Self-Hosted: https://{your-domain}/mcp ... MCP connections inherit the permissions of the authenticated user (OAuth) or the role assigned to the API key.
s9.title: MCP Server - Twenty Documentation

limitations: MCP server is currently in alpha and may not be enabled for all workspaces yet.
The schema-per-tenant API means there is no static API reference — endpoints are generated per workspace.
Rate limits (50-100 req/min) may be restrictive for some agent workloads.
Could not verify if Composio has a dedicated Twenty tool in its catalog.
